author: DDD
content: DDD update, May 10: Constant Contact sender settings still need their own confirmation: sender address, display name, and reply-to address. The current Resend/DKIM blocker applies to Dev Dashboard sending and does not by itself confirm or block the Constant Contact configuration.

author: DDD
content: DDD update, May 10: info@ mailbox access is confirmed through gws-info. If info@ is the intended reply inbox, the team can define reply-monitoring ownership, cadence, and escalation separately from the IONOS/DKIM work. The current DNS blocker affects authenticated sending through Resend, not basic inbox access.

author: DDD
content:
DDD update, May 10: info@ mailbox access is confirmed, and the Resend invite has been accepted. Supabase already has a RESEND_API_KEY secret from May 8, so key rotation is not needed unless later testing proves the key is invalid.

The Dev Dashboard send path was tested. The app-side Supabase auth issue has a local code fix: send-email calls now include Supabase auth headers and the existing app token. With auth in place, the function reaches Resend.

Current blocker: Resend is rejecting sends because thenorthstarhouse.org is not domain-verified. SPF/MX records are verified, but the IONOS TXT value for resend._domainkey does not match the DKIM value shown in Resend.

Access blocker: IONOS login routes through admin@thenorthstarhouse.org. That mailbox is currently blocked by Google 2FA to a phone we do not control. Next step is to recover admin mailbox/2FA access, or have the DNS/account owner replace the existing resend._domainkey TXT value, restart Resend verification, and retest sending.
